meta_title: For Students: Antigravity project template to Solve can't collaborate (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome can't collaborate. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Antigravity Template to Solve Collaboration
content.seo_description: Master collaborative coding as a student. Use the Antigravity template and Claude Code to break down silos and ship your first professional-grade AI project.
content.answer_block: Collaboration paralysis ends by standardizing your environment. By using the Antigravity project template, you synchronize dependencies, coding standards, and deployment paths instantly. Paired with Claude Code, this setup transforms your isolated student project into a professional-grade codebase, allowing you to iterate faster and demonstrate production-ready skills to future employers.
content.intro:
You stare at the blank terminal, the weight of a 'solo' project looming. You've got the vision, but when you try to invite others or integrate complex features, the codebase fractures. The isolation of student development often leads to fragile architectures that break the moment you try to scale or collaborate.

But the era of the 'lone wolf' coder is over. By adopting the Antigravity project template, you aren't just writing scripts; you are engineering a modular, collaborative ecosystem. This is the bridge between writing code that 'just works' and building software that scales in a professional team environment.

steps.title: Initialize the Antigravity Foundation
steps.description: Establish the root directory structure that enforces modularity, ensuring your code remains decoupled for future team contributions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a new project scaffold using the Antigravity standard template with support for TypeScript and modular API routing.

steps.title: Configure Shared Environment Context
steps.description: Sync your environment variables and dependency versions to prevent 'it works on my machine' syndrome during peer reviews.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a standardized .env.example and dependency lock file that ensures cross-machine consistency for my AI agent backend.

steps.title: Implement Collaborative Linting Rules
steps.description: Set strict, automated code quality checks so every contributor writes in the same style, reducing friction during pull requests.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure ESLint and Prettier rules aligned with the Antigravity professional style guide to enforce clean, readable code.

steps.title: Build Modular Agentic Features
steps.description: Use Claude Code to wrap your core logic in isolated functions, making it trivial for others to contribute without stepping on your toes.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the core logic into modular, testable service classes that follow the Antigravity service-provider pattern.

steps.title: Deploy the Collaborative Gateway
steps.description: Set up a CI/CD pipeline that automatically validates new code, ensuring the project remains stable as your team grows.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a GitHub Actions workflow that runs the full test suite and verifies build stability on every push.

pro_tips: Always commit your lockfiles; they are the source of truth for your dependencies across different machines.
Use Claude Code to document your functions as you write them; uncommented code is technical debt that kills team velocity.
Adopt a 'Branch-per-Feature' strategy even in solo projects to build the muscle memory for professional Git workflows.
Leverage the Antigravity template's built-in testing harness to write unit tests before you write the actual feature logic.
Keep your API keys out of the repo by utilizing the Antigravity secret management hooks during the initial setup.
content.next_steps: Once your environment is synced, invite a peer to clone the repo and attempt to run the test suite. If they can build it in under 5 minutes, your template is successful. Then, begin adding your first feature branch.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools, the template, and the roadmap. Stop coding in a vacuum and start building software that is ready for the real world. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
